Dear Mr. Oralo s 3
Thank you for meeting with me in your offices on Friday,
August 7, 1992, to discuss the 1992 ground rules for operation
Cobbte, a program which has been describeit in previous
correspondence. As we discusseal, the California water
associition ("CwA" ) is once again poisecl to distribute
thousanals of holiclay turkeys to neealy families throughout
California, assuming that we can agree upon reasonable
timitations which will permit the charitable gifts to be
recognized as such for feileral and state tax purposes. As we
have atso discussed, to the extent that a contribution is
deemecl by tax authorities be a political contribution, it is
J.ike1y to be regarded as having been macle with a political
rather than a donative intent. 
In any event, C9{A is obviously
not engageil in anything other than a charitable endeavor and
can clo much more with the incentive ancl aid of tax laws
recognizing charitable contributions.
At our meeting, you expressed a great aleal of sympathy for
the program. We both agreeal that the five "conditions" agreeal
to between the FPPC anal CwA in 1991 remain viable and should be
re-adopteil as conilitions for the 1992 program to avoid the
making of inadvertent potitical contributions to legislators
who may support the Program.


NOSSAMAN, CUTHNER, KNOX 8 ELLIOTT
Mr. Wayne Ordos
August L2, L992
RE: Operation GobbIe
Page 2
In my JuIy 2, l-992, correspondence with Mr. Davidian, I
raised the additional problem of comments which were aclcled to
the 1991 conalitions in a letter from Mr. Daviclian to me. (See
Aalkisson Advice Letter (N. A-9I-512; Nov. 18, 1992.) Mr.
Daviclian hail cormenteil that the conditions might or might not
be sufficient for the 1992 season and suggesteal that "it would
be better" if CwA did not rely on legislators to help designate
worthy charities or consult with Iegislators about scheduled
events at which the holiday turkeys are to be given away.
As I emphasized in my JuIy 2, 1992, letter, it would be
inappropriate to bar a legislator from designating a worthy
charity or from being consulted about an event, unless the
circumstances resulteal in either a representation that the
source of the donation was the elected official, 
or in an
exclusive forum for the elected official 
aimed at aiding in
election efforts.
Since these events are schecluleal for after the election
cycIe, and the original five conclitions already prohibit an
exclusive forum or the crediting of a alonation to an electeal
official, 
any additional bar to free communication with an
elected official 
is unnecessary to avoid a political
contribution. 
since the aalalitional conalitions are unnecessary,
they would, in my view, constitute overreaching by the
Commission anal would unnecessarily interfere with CwA's and its
members' rights of speech anal association.
Of equal importance, as we cliscussed, the additional
conditions may, as a practical matter, jeopardize the program,
given the history of involvement of legislators anal the risk
that such broad conilitions would result in every legislator
reporting the turkeys as political contributions even in
circumstances where such reporting is unnecessary. In short,
we strongly urge you to permit the 1992 program to go forwaral
without adtling adclitional barriers such as those eluded to in
the Chairman's 1991, advice letter.
During our discussion we also agreeil that two aclditional
safeguards might serve to better protect the integrity of the
charitable program. First, you requesteil an outline of the
program questionnaire packet which wiIl be utilizecl to monitor
and administer Operation cobble in 1992. I have attached a
JDA/jw 756


'NOSSAMAN, CUTHNER, KNOX 8 ELLIOTT
Mr. wayne Ordos
August 12, L992
RE: Operation Gobble
Page 3
rough outline of the packet to this correspondence. The
purpose of the packet is to maintain uniformity in the program
throughout California so that we may iilentify any rare
instances in which the conditions have EqL been met. In such
instances, we intenil to recommend to legislators that they
report the value of the turkeys as in*kinal political
contributions. 
Hopefully, however, through uniformity, the
charitable intent of each gift can be maintained by strict
aclherence to the conditions.
The second safeguard which you suggesteal at our meeting is
also acceptable. You requesteal that a listing of the times and
locations of the events be provicled in advance of the events,
presumably to facilitate 
iliscretionary observance by FPPC
officials or members of the general public. 
I am informed by
leaders within my client's organization that this should pose
no problem.
I have hacl a difficult 
time, however, with a third request
you maale at our meeting. Essentially, you requestecl that I
compile a sunmary of the aclvice I gave to my client regartling
specific events and legislators during the 1991 season. Upon
further reflection I must clecline because of the waiver of
attorney-client privilege involved in such a summary. I have
not been able to devise a way to share these details without
inaclvertently compromising my client's right to private counsel
anal my firm's integrity in its srork product. I would be more
than happy to discuss the general nature of my oversight for
1991 and 1992. However, I have conclualed that specific
reporting of my advice anal communications would serve no
purpose.
I would appreciate a response by no later than the end of
August, since my client plans to convene a meeting to authorize
and plan the 1992 program in early September.
Sincerely,
JDA/jw 756
& ELLIOTT

sections.question_synthetic: The letter addresses conditions and safeguards for Operation Gobble, a charitable turkey distribution program by the California Water Association, and discusses whether additional restrictions proposed by the FPPC are necessary to avoid characterizing the gifts as political contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming letter from counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. No formal question or conclusion sections present. Document references an earlier FPPC Advice Letter (A-91-512) but this letter itself is advocacy correspondence from private counsel rather than FPPC guidance.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The letter addresses conditions and safeguards for Operation Gobble, a charitable turkey distribution program by the California Water Association, and discusses whether additional restrictions proposed by the FPPC are necessary to avoid characterizing the gifts as political contributions.

embedding.summary: This is correspondence from counsel to the California Water Association to FPPC staff regarding Operation Gobble, a charitable holiday turkey distribution program. The letter discusses previously agreed conditions, disputes additional proposed restrictions, and proposes two safeguards (a program questionnaire packet and advance event listing) while declining to provide a summary of prior legal advice due to attorney-client privilege.
